skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
skill.summary: A git workflow skill that verifies tests pass, presents 4 completion options, and executes the chosen workflow for merging, PR creation, or branch cleanup.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The 23 external command detections are legitimate git operations (checkout, branch, merge, push, worktree) that form the core functionality of this git workflow skill. The 2 filesystem detections are standard shell redirection and git worktree management. The high-severity crypto flags are false positives from text pattern misidentification.

content.value_statement: This skill automates the final steps of feature development by verifying tests pass, presenting clear merge/PR options, and executing the chosen workflow safely.
actual_capabilities: Verify all tests pass before presenting completion options
Present exactly 4 structured options: merge locally, create PR, keep branch, or discard
Execute git merge to base branch with verification
Push branch and create GitHub pull request with formatted template
Safely cleanup git worktrees after branch completion
Require explicit confirmation before discarding work
limitations: Requires gh CLI installed for PR creation
Does not handle merge conflicts automatically
Does not support non-GitHub remote providers

use_cases.title: Complete a feature branch
use_cases.description: After finishing implementation tasks, use this skill to cleanly merge or PR the completed work
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on feature branches

use_cases.title: Clean up development worktrees
use_cases.description: Use after development is done to remove temporary worktrees created during development
use_cases.target_user: Developers using git worktrees for parallel development

use_cases.title: Standardize team branch completion
use_cases.description: Ensure consistent branch completion workflow across team members

prompt_templates.title: Basic branch completion
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm done with my feature. Can you help me finish this branch?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has completed implementation and wants to complete the branch

prompt_templates.title: Create PR and cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: All tests are passing. I want to create a pull request and clean up my worktree.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to push branch and create PR with cleanup

prompt_templates.title: Merge locally with verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Please merge my branch into main locally and verify tests still pass.
prompt_templates.scenario: User prefers local merge over PR workflow

prompt_templates.title: Safe branch discard
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to discard this work. Please confirm before deleting anything.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to abandon branch with safety confirmation

output_examples.input: I'm done with my feature. Can you help me finish this branch?
output_examples.output:
Running test suite...

✓ All tests pass (42 passed, 0 failed)

Implementation complete. What would you like to do?

1. Merge back to main locally
2. Push and create a Pull Request
3. Keep the branch as-is (I'll handle it later)
4. Discard this work

Which option?

✓ Pushed branch 'feature/add-user-auth'
✓ Created PR #42: Add user authentication
✓ Removed worktree at '/Users/dev/project/.git/worktrees/feature-add-user-auth'

Branch complete! Pull request created and worktree cleaned up.

best_practices: Always verify tests pass before presenting options to prevent merging broken code
Present exactly 4 options without additional explanation to keep user choice clear
Require typed 'discard' confirmation to prevent accidental work loss
anti_patterns: Skipping test verification and proceeding directly to merge or PR
Asking open-ended questions like 'What should I do next?' instead of presenting options
Automatically cleaning up worktrees for all options (only do for merge and discard)

faq.question: What happens if tests fail?
faq.answer: The skill stops and displays test failures. It will not proceed to the options step until tests pass.

faq.question: Can I create a PR without pushing first?
faq.answer: No. Option 2 requires pushing the branch to origin before creating the PR.

faq.question: What is a worktree?
faq.answer: A git worktree lets you check out multiple branches simultaneously in separate directories.

faq.question: Does this skill handle merge conflicts?
faq.answer: No. The skill will report conflicts and stop. You must resolve them manually.

faq.question: Which git hosts are supported?
faq.answer: PR creation requires GitHub and the gh CLI. Other hosts work for local merge only.

faq.question: What if I choose Option 3 (keep branch)?
faq.answer: The branch and worktree are preserved. No cleanup occurs. You handle it manually later.
